description_long: This object is a cylindrical, repurposed metal coffee can used as an ashtray, filled with a dark, ashy substance and several cigarette butts protruding from the top. The can is heavily weathered, with visible rust, grime, and faded branding, including the recognizable 'Folgers' coffee logo partially visible on one side. The rim is slightly uneven and the interior is filled with a mixture of ash, cigarette remnants, and possibly some liquid residue. The exterior shows signs of prolonged outdoor exposure, with streaks of corrosion and discoloration. The object is utilitarian, likely found in urban environments such as sidewalks, bus stops, or near building entrances. Its size and construction suggest it is portable but sturdy enough to withstand frequent use.
description: A weathered, rusted metal coffee can repurposed as an ashtray, filled with ash and cigarette butts, featuring faded Folgers branding. The object is utilitarian and shows signs of heavy outdoor use.
description_view_0: The can's rim and ashy contents with several cigarette butts are clearly visible, with the rusted metal surface and no clear branding in this view.
description_view_1: This view prominently displays the faded 'Folgers' logo and more of the can's weathered, rusted exterior, along with the ashy interior and cigarette butts.
description_view_2: The side of the can shows partial branding and heavy corrosion, with the ashy contents and cigarette butts still visible.
description_view_3: This angle highlights the most worn and faded section of the can, with less visible branding and a clear view of the ashy, cigarette-filled interior.
